risk_title: Security-driven project suspensions affecting implementation timelines
risk_summary: Project implementation has experienced multiple security-related suspensions causing substantial delays. Lot 1 was suspended from November 2021 to April 2023 (17 months) due to security issues, with actual work execution not resuming until April 2024. Lot 3 was suspended from November 2023 to November 2024 due to security problems. These suspensions have cumulatively resulted in 128 months of time loss since project effectiveness.
evidence_quote: The project was suspended due to security issues from November 2021 until the contractor remobilized in April 2023... Although the contractor resumed works in April 2023, the actual execution did not start until April 2024 due to the re-establishment and re-mobilization of resources.

risk_title: Lot 2 contractor demobilization due to force majeure with uncertain remobilization
risk_summary: Lot 2 contractor has been demobilized since November 2021 (over 3 years) due to force majeure/security events. While security situation has reportedly improved, contractor remobilization remains uncertain and dependent on ongoing negotiations. Contractor facilities and traffic signs have been vandalized during the suspension period.
evidence_quote: The project was suspended in November 2021, affecting the completion of the remaining works... the contractor's facilities and traffic signs have been vandalized by unknown groups... ERA is negotiating with the contractor to remobilize to the site as soon as possible.

risk_title: Persistent security challenges affecting project site operations and staff safety
risk_summary: Despite reported improvements, security remains a persistent concern affecting project implementation. The document notes that security challenges previously impacted implementation and that ongoing engagement with security offices is required to ensure continuous improvement of security situations at project sites.
evidence_quote: Despite security challenges previously, the project implementation has shown momentum with the attention given to the project by the Government... ERA to continue engaging with the relevant security office at the appropriate level and ensure continuous improvement of the security situation in the respective sites

risk_title: Vandalism and infrastructure damage during security-related suspensions
risk_summary: During project suspension periods, project infrastructure and facilities have been damaged by unknown groups. Lot 2 experienced vandalism of contractor facilities and traffic signs during the November 2021 suspension period, indicating vulnerability of project assets to security-related threats.
evidence_quote: the contractor's facilities and traffic signs have been vandalized by unknown groups

risk_title: Weak grievance redress mechanism functionality indicating limited community engagement
risk_summary: The Grievance Redress Mechanism (GRM) established for Project Affected Persons shows weak functionality with only 6 complaints reported since the last mission, suggesting either limited community awareness, trust issues, or inadequate accessibility. This weak GRM could mask underlying community tensions or grievances related to land acquisition and compensation.
evidence_quote: weak functionality of Grievance Redress Mechanism (GRM) as only 6 complaints related to roadside erosion and dust issues were reported

risk_title: Limited stakeholder consultations affecting social cohesion and project acceptance
risk_summary: Stakeholder consultations have been limited across the three project lots, with only 39 participants engaged since the last mission. This limited engagement could undermine community buy-in and increase tensions, particularly given the project's significant land acquisition impact (7,216 households affected).
evidence_quote: limited stakeholders' consultations are conducted across the three lots (39 participants) since the last mission

risk_title: Uncertainty regarding additional land acquisition and potential displacement
risk_summary: The mission was informed that there could be additional land acquisition requirements for the project. The need to prepare RAP addendums prior to additional land acquisition indicates potential for further displacement and compensation disputes, particularly given the already substantial impact on 7,216 households.
evidence_quote: Mission was informed that there could be additional land acquisition in the project. Mission suggested to prepare a brief addendum to the RAP prior to land acquisition and physical and/or economical relocation of PAPs for any additional land requirement.
